title: Income Tax Appeal Bangalore | Virtual Auditor — CA V. Viswanathan
description: Income tax appeal services in Bangalore. CIT(A), ITAT representation. Expert CA handling assessment disputes and demand reduction. Call +91-95139 39333.
bodyText:
Looking for income tax appeal services in Bangalore? Virtual Auditor handles income tax appeals at all levels — CIT(A), ITAT Bangalore Bench, and High Court — led by CA V. Viswanathan (FCA, ACS). From assessment order analysis through appeal filing and hearing representation, we fight to reduce or eliminate unjust demands.

What is Income Tax Appeal Bangalore | Virtual Auditor — CA V. Viswanathan?

  HEADING2: What We Deliver
  Detailed analysis of assessment order and computation of demand. Appeal grounds preparation with legal citations, case law references, and factual submissions. Form 35 filing with CIT(A) or Form 36 filing with ITAT within statutory timelines. Written submissions with comprehensive legal arguments supported by Supreme Court and High Court precedents. Virtual hearing representation before CIT(A) through the ITBA e-proceedings portal. Physical appearance at ITAT Bangalore Bench when required. Stay of demand application (Form 35A / stay petition) to prevent coercive recovery during appeal pendency. Post-appeal advisory — further appeal to ITAT/High Court if CIT(A) order is unfavourable.
  HEADING2: Why Choose Virtual Auditor for Income Tax Appeals in Bangalore
  Income tax appeals require both technical expertise in tax law and practical experience with appellate proceedings. CA V. Viswanathan has represented clients across assessment disputes including Section 68 unexplained credits, Section 69/69A unexplained investments, transfer pricing adjustments, capital gains disputes, and disallowance of deductions under Chapter VI-A. Our Bangalore office provides local support for ITAT Bangalore Bench hearings. We prepare appeal grounds with current case law — citing relevant ITAT, High Court, and Supreme Court decisions that support your position. Success in tax appeals depends on the quality of legal argumentation, not just factual presentation.
  HEADING2: Our Process
  Step 1: Assessment order review — identify grounds of appeal, compute disputed demand, assess merits. Step 2: Stay of demand application if recovery proceedings are imminent. Step 3: Appeal filing (Form 35/36) within 30 days of assessment order. Step 4: Written submissions preparation with legal research and case law. Step 5: Hearing representation — virtual or physical. Step 6: Follow-up on appeal order and advisory on further remedies.
  HEADING2: Get Started Today
  Facing an income tax demand in Bangalore? Contact us:
  Call/WhatsApp: +91-95139 39333
  Email: support@virtualauditor.in
  Visit: 7th Floor, Mahalakshmi Chambers, 29, MG Road, Bangalore 560001
  Free assessment order review. No-obligation consultation.

faqs.q: What is the time limit for filing income tax appeal?
faqs.a: Appeal to CIT(A): 30 days from date of assessment order. Appeal to ITAT: 60 days from CIT(A) order. Condonation of delay is possible with valid reasons but not guaranteed — timely filing is critical.

faqs.q: Can I get a stay on income tax demand during appeal?
faqs.a: Yes. You can apply for stay of demand under Section 220(6) to the Assessing Officer, or file a stay petition with CIT(A)/ITAT. Generally, payment of 20% of disputed demand is expected for granting stay (CBDT Circular).

faqs.q: What are the fees for income tax appeal in Bangalore?
faqs.a: Appeal fees depend on the complexity of issues, number of assessment years, and disputed amount. We offer a free initial review of your assessment order. Contact +91-95139 39333 for a fixed-fee quote after review.
